title: Thelypteris paraphysophora
taxonomy: Thelypteris paraphysophora is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes many other species of ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is further divided into several genera, and Thelypteris is one of the most well-known. Thelypteris paraphysophora is native to various regions, including Central and South America, and is often found in tropical and subtropical habitats. Its classification within the plant kingdom is as a vascular plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Thelypteris paraphysophora has been reported to have medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America. However, specific information on its medicinal uses is limited. More studies are required to fully understand the medicinal potential of Thelypteris paraphysophora.

intro: Thelypteris paraphysophora, also known as the Narrow-leaved Maidenhair Fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris paraphysophora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
